name: David Heinemeier Hansson
tagline: Rails, startups, and contrarian tech takes
description: Exploring DHH's writings on Ruby on Rails, software development, big tech, remote work, and entrepreneurship.

llmAnalysis.contextPrompt: You are analyzing a blog post by David Heinemeier Hansson (DHH), creator of Ruby on Rails and co-founder of 37signals (Basecamp/HEY). He is known for strong, contrarian opinions on software development, remote work, big tech, open source, and entrepreneurship.

spiciness.contextPrompt: DHH is known for provocative, contrarian takes that challenge industry consensus. He argues against microservices, cloud computing, TypeScript, and venture capital while championing monoliths, owning your hardware, Ruby, and bootstrapped businesses. His writing style is deliberately confrontational.
